notes: This index is a derived, rebuildable projection over finding bundles and ArtifactRecords.
It is not a governance gate, runtime surface, readiness surface, or promotion surface.

items.subject: ri_policy_router
items.summary: The aged weak continuation guard worsened both December fail windows and did not remove the intended late-December losing seam.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat the late-December losing rows as one homogeneous aged weak continuation seam.
Do not advance this candidate to keep-set or stress-set verification.

items.subject: ri_policy_router
items.summary: The late-December continuation issue is split across two distinct seam classes, so the next candidate must choose one target instead of bundling both together.
do_not_repeat: Do not bundle seam A and seam B into one weak-continuation-only candidate.
Do not touch already-strong continuation semantics without a newly scoped packet.

items.subject: ri_policy_router
items.summary: The weak pre-aged release seam at 2023-12-22 15:00 is reachable and can be blocked by a bounded continuation-local guard.
do_not_repeat: Do not claim seam A is unreachable or untestable.
Do not reopen seam discovery for 2023-12-22 15:00 without citing this reachability result.

items.subject: ri_policy_router
items.summary: The weak pre-aged release candidate hits the intended seam-A row but still worsens both fail-set windows through broader action churn.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat 'target row hit' as sufficient evidence of improvement.
Do not advance this candidate to keep-set or stress-set verification in its current form.

items.subject: ri_policy_router
items.summary: The seam-A candidate fails through repeated no-trade chaining plus a recurring two-bar cooldown displacement loop, not because the intended row was missed.
do_not_repeat: Do not frame the next seam-A candidate as a simple one-row veto.
Do not ignore repeated previous_policy equals RI_no_trade_policy chaining when designing the next seam-A candidate.

items.subject: ri_policy_router
items.summary: Substituted-continuation weakness does not follow one universal annual shape on the curated observational surface: 2020 concentrates bad rows into a few acute windows, 2018 and 2022 revisit recurrent multi-row weak windows, and 2025 is weaker in a more fragmented form.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat continuation weakness as one universal positive-versus-negative annual rule.
Do not collapse acute 2020 concentration and recurrent 2018 or 2022 multi-window weakness into the same local shape.
Do not reopen runtime tuning directly from this local-window packaging finding.

items.subject: ri_policy_router
items.summary: Even inside fixed mixed local windows, blocked-versus-substituted balance is phase-sensitive rather than universal: blocked rows look locally stronger in the mixed March 2020 window but locally weaker in the recurrent hostile March 2018 window.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat same-window overlap as row-matched proof of universal blocked-versus-substituted superiority.
Do not widen to more same-window subjects before resolving phase ordering on the existing 2020 and 2018 windows.
Do not use these two fixed windows as runtime tuning or promotion authority.
